Now, because we don't have the possibility to access the backend, we used the browser local storage, the advantages being :

Persistent storage across sessions and tabs

Keeps data even if the user closes and reopens the browser.

Extension-wide access

Unlike localStorage, which is tab-specific (per origin), chrome.storage.local is accessible from anywhere in the extension - background scripts, popups, etc.

Asynchronous, non-blocking API

Especially important in our case, where you don’t want to freeze the UI or block the background script, for example the recurring jobs.

Good capacity (5MB limit)

Plenty for what we’re doing — storing timestamps and intervals per tenant will take kilobytes at most.

Isolation from page scripts

A big security win: web pages cannot access yourchrome.storage.local.

Do I really need to set a refresh interval after installing the extension ?

In theory no, because we have a default interval of 1440 minutes set, but since this is the initial version, we would recommend setting one.

What happens at the initial load ?

The script will iterate through your packages and each integration artifact, search for ProcessDirect links and save them in your browser in-memory storage.

What happens when the schedule auto-refresh happens?

Same thing, only that you won’t get bothered by the logs, since it will be done in the background. You could still see the logs in the browser Console.

What happens if I close the pop-up when the initial load is running ?

Process should still run, and if you re-open the extension, you should be able the logs flowing through, if the process has not ended yet.

What if a flow gets an update and the addresses changes or the flow step gets completely deleted ?

The extension will remove old values and add the new ones.

Is any personal data accessed & shared ?

No and no. Only you will be able to see the logs/saved values.

What if I want to suggest improvements/report a bug ?

Currently we have a form created, and it's accessible via a link added in the extension. Of course, you can message us in private, comment here, doesn't matter. As long as we get feedbacks, we will try to make it as better as possible.

 

Planned future improvements

We are looking into multiple options to extend the current functionalities, like :

- WhereUsed for security artifact names found in flows.

- Extending the visual map to highlight the whole process chain, so not for the ones that share the same ProcessDirect address, but from start to the end.

- Reports on unused ProcessDirect addresses.

 

Downloading the extension

The extension is available currently only on Chrome extension store, so you can go ahead and get it from there. In the future, we will publish the project to Github as well.

 

Bear in mind that the extension shares absolutely no data, does not collect anything and it is not monetized. It is created strictly for the benefit of the integration developers and will always stay like this.

If you have any questions, feel free to reply here, or even go to the dedicated Q&A Section for SAP Integration Suite.

Thank you for your time !

entry.title: SAP Developer News April 17th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/04/17/sap-developer-news-april-17th-2025/

Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes and UI5 Renovate Preset Config Tool

  • SAP Fiori Belize Theme Removal Announcement: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/announcement-removal-of-belize-theme-of-sap-fiori/ba-p/14061924
  • Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/upcoming-removal-of-sap-fiori-themes-belize-and-blue-crystal/ba-p/14063111
  • UI5 Renovate Preset Config Tool: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/stay-up-to-date-with-the-ui5-renovate-preset-config/ba-p/14070649

New SQL on Files Use Cases with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 01

  • Exploring New SQL on Files Use Cases with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024 and QRC 01/2025: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/exploring-new-sql-on-files-use-cases-with-sap-hana-cloud-qrc-04-2024-and/ba-p/14076223
  • Tutorials to Explore SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-dbx-sof.html

SAP BTP Cloud Foundry: switching to higher security level Root Certificate Authority

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-cloud-foundry-switching-to-higher-security-level-root-certificate/ba-p/14061965

Migration Tooling now supporting migration via the pipeline approach

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/introducing-the-new-project-creation-wizard-within-sap-build-lobby/ba-p/14079038

 

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal

0:53 UI5 Renovate Preset Config Tool

1:41 New SQL on Files Use Cases with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 01

2:59 SAP BTP Cloud Foundry: switching to higher security level Root Certificate Authority

3:46 Migration Tooling now supporting migration via the pipeline approach

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Into] This is the SAP Developer News for April 10th, 2025.

[Michelle] SAP has announced the removal of the Belize and Blue Crystal themes from its Fiori user interface, starting in the later half of this 2025. Belize along with Blue Crystal, will be phased out to make way for more modern themes like Quartz and Horizon These new themes promise enhanced design and usability, giving users a more streamlined interface SAP urges users to transition to these new themes to benefit from current and future improvements. By embracing Quartz and Horizon, organizations can ensure they remain aligned with SAP's innovation focus. if you want to learn more about the removal of these themes and to prepare for it. checkout the links below. In other news, SAP has introduced the UI5 Renovate Preset Config a tool designed to keep SAP UI5 projects effortlessly up-to-date. It automates the dependency update process, allowing developers and organizations to remain current with the latest framework improvements and security patches. The UI5 Renovate Preset Config minimizes manual intervention saving time while improving application stability This tool is an efficient and straightforward solution for developers eager to enhance project reliability with regular updates! for more information check below for link to blog post about this new tool. As always, thanks for watching and happy coding!

[Witalij] The SQL on files capability in SAP HANA Cloud has unlock new potential by providing direct read-only SQL access to files stored in SAP HANA Cloud and data lake files since QRC03 2024. Starting with the QRC 04 2024 of SAP HANA Cloud, this capability has been further extended to support direct access to the most common external object storages in addition to SAP HANA Cloud-owned data lake files, namely Amazon S3, Azure Blob Storage or ADLS Gen2, and Google Cloud Storage. With the ability to access vast volumes of data from managed object storage, you can fully leverage the robust features of SAP HANA Cloud as a platform. This includes in-memory processing, machine learning capabilities, predictive analytics library, and so forth. Another piece of good news is that the existing limitations on delta tables, which previously supported only Reader version 1 for both direct file access and delta sharing, has been lifted starting with the SAP HANA Cloud QRC01 this year. For hands-on exercises, check the updated tutorials at Explore SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on files.

[Mamikee] The SAP process orchestration to SAP integration suite now support automatic creation of integration artifacts supporting the pipeline for cloud integration. This update simplifies the shots from SAP process orchestration by using a pattern based method that automatically generate integration artifacts and makes your integration flow cleaner, smarter, and way easier to manage. Now, the pipeline model offers better error handling, fewer queues to juggle, and reusable components that save you time. No more stretching over rigid templates. In fact, it identifies supported patterns, making more scenarios eligible for migration. To learn more about this, check the link in the description below.

[DJ] If you're a user of the SAP Business Technology platform, whether using applications or services offered by SAP or by others, this is an important item, especially if you manage trust certificates yourself. There's a change to the root certificate authority, CA, used to issue certificates on SAP domains for Cloud Foundry, basically the TLS handshake between your browser and the server. This is for environments on BTP. This change is due to a recommendation from the Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik, that's the German Federal Office for Information Security. In essence, there's an old G2 certificate, the DigiCert Global Route CA, and that's being replaced by a newer DigiCert TLS RSA 4096 Route G5. You can manage Route Certificate Authority certificates yourself, or, and this is what we recommend because it makes it a lot easier, you can use the BTP Trust Store. In any case, you must make sure that your trust store does contain this new G5 certificate, especially if you do manage the trust store yourself. Anyway, for all the details, check out the blog post that's linked in the description.

entry.title: Read Lock strategy to consider while moving sftp / file adapter to BTP Cloud Integration.
entry.content:
Read Lock strategy to consider while moving sftp / file adapter with – “Msecs to wait before Modification check” while migrating to BTP – Cloud Integration.

SAP Process Integration (PI) offers a "File Modification Check" that enables adapters to delay file processing for a defined period, ensuring only complete files are handled. A comparable feature in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Cloud Integration would significantly enhance file-based integration. While many file transfer scenarios necessitate source system handling to guarantee fully written files before processing, the SFTP adapter currently lacks a built-in option to specify such a waiting period.

SAP BTP Cloud Integration offers several options for implementing a read lock strategy with the SFTP adapter

 

These Includes:

Done File Expected: This strategy requires a "done file" to signal when a file is ready for processing. If this option is selected, the name of the .Done file must be provided. The .done file serves as a signal that the file is ready for processing and must be located in the same folder as the file to be processed.

 

Content Change: Monitors changes in the file length/modification timestamp to determine if the write operation on the file is complete and the file is ready to be read. With this option selected, the system waits for at least one second until there are no more file changes.

While this method can detect some changes, it is unreliable when the file is updated slowly, as the system may miss changes if they don’t occur within the fixed time. This limitation makes it unsuitable for scenarios where files are being written gradually or slowly.

Rename: This strategy renames the file on the SFTP server before reading it.

None: This picks up the file as soon as it pools. There is a lot of chance that a incomplete or half-baked file might be picked up.

 

Now to avoid picking up of half-baked / incomplete file in PI/PO (7.5) file adapter there was below option:

 

 

However, with ‘Content Change’ Read lock strategy option of BTP – CI Sftp adapter possess various disadvantages

  1. Unlike PI you can’t fix how much time to wait, and it normally waits for a second, which can result in incomplete files being picked in case of large files written via slow network.
  2. It also doesn’t pick zero length file, if that is required due to business requirement.

See - https://me.sap.com/notes/3347842/E

 

Alternatives to consider:

  1. Write done file: if the sender application can write any .done or any other Done file. The important point to remember is the done file will be deleted on successful completion of iflow.
  2. Timing the sftp adapter to pick file in certain time slots which is different from time slot used by source to write file. Interval in Time slot between source writing a file and Sftp adapter picking the file will mostly ensure no incomplete files are picked.

There might be other suitable alternatives which has not been discussed here. Kindly comment below.

Here is another fine blog which explains things in detail:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/read-lock-strategy-and-post-processing-options-in-sftp-sender-adapter-of/ba-p/13552916

entry.title: How to migrate Boomi Integrations to SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
1. Purpose of the document

This blog provides a comprehensive guide on migrating SAP SuccessFactors HCM integrations from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite (SAP IS), highlighting its strategic advantages, including prepackaged integrations, Tech Envo Accelerators, Migration Framework, Migration Process,  Design considerations, and hybrid deployment options. It covers SAP’s runtime environments, deployment models, and TechEnvo’s migration accelerators, ensuring a seamless migration. With TechEnvo’s expertise and adherence to SAP best practices, organizations can modernize their integration landscape while ensuring scalability, consistency, and long-term success.

 

2. Why SAP SuccessFactors HCM customer needs to migrate from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite

  • Between 2013 and 2016, SAP SuccessFactors HCM customers used BOOMI as an integration platform to support data integration requirements in SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation, and many customers are still running on Boomi.
  • SAP Customers have significantly invested in SAP BTP and SAP Integration Suite in the last seven years as part of S4HANA transitions and Integration Modernization (moving from SAP PO to SAP IS). 
  • Customers are migrating interfaces from Boomi to the SAP Integration Suite, aligning with the SAP Integration strategy.
  • Maintaining multiple integration platforms adds a lot of Technical Debt, and SAP customers consolidate and move to one integration platform (SAP Integration Suite) 
  • SAP Integration Suite Capabilities on par with BOOMI

3. SAP Integration Suite Capabilities and Features

SAP Integration Suite (SAP IS) is the strategic integration platform for SAP customers, offering seamless connectivity with over 3,400 prebuilt integrations and 3,800+ APIs. It provides all the capabilities of Boomi and additional advantages such as prepackaged content covering most of the SAP Solutions Integrations, AI-driven integration, and enhanced security. 

SAP Integration Suite is a comprehensive set of tools and services that enable seamless integration across various SAP and third-party systems. It helps businesses connect, automate, and optimize their processes across cloud and on-premises environments. Here are some key features and components of the SAP Integration Suite:

3.1 Key Features:

  • Connectivity: Provides pre-built connectors and adapters for integrating various SAP and third-party applications, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and external applications like Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamics.
  • Integration Flows: Facilitates the orchestration of data between applications with customizable integration flows, enabling businesses to automate workflows and synchronize data.
  • API Management: Allows businesses to manage their APIs in a secure and scalable manner, with tools to create, expose, and govern APIs across multiple environments.
  • Data Transformation: Supports the transformation of data between different formats (e.g., XML, JSON) to ensure seamless communication between systems.
  • Monitoring and Analytics: Provides dashboards and monitoring tools for real-time visibility into integration processes and system health, allowing proactive management.
  • Cloud-Based: Being cloud-native, SAP Integration Suite provides flexibility and scalability to handle evolving integration needs.
  • Security: Offers robust security features, including authentication, authorization, encryption, and secure communication for data exchanges

3.2 SAP Integration Suite Core Components:

  1. SAP Cloud Integration (CPI): A cloud-based integration platform for connecting SAP and non-SAP applications. It allows seamless integration of cloud and on-premise systems.
  2. SAP API Management: A solution to design, publish, and monitor APIs, providing tools to securely expose and consume APIs.
  3. SAP Open Connectors: A collection of pre-built connectors to a variety of third-party applications and services.
  4. SAP Integration Advisor: A tool that automates the mapping and transformation of data across systems, helping streamline the integration process.
  5. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): While the Integration Suite is a part of BTP, it works closely with other BTP services like SAP HANA, SAP AI, and SAP analytics for a comprehensive digital transformation solution.

3.3 SAP Integration Suite Use Cases:

  • ERP Integration: Connect SAP SuccessFactors HCM and SAP S/4HANA with other enterprise systems, such as CRM, Payroll, and Time, Benefits..etc, to ensure a unified view of business operations.
  • Cloud and On-Premises Integration: Link cloud-based applications (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors HCM ) with on-premises systems (e.g., SAP ECC).
  • API-First Strategy: Enable businesses to expose services as APIs and integrate them with external systems for broader connectivity.
  • Data Synchronization: Automatically sync data between systems, ensuring consistency and eliminating manual data entry errors.

3.4 SAP Integration Suite Benefits:

  • Faster Time-to-Value: Pre-built integrations and connectors reduce implementation time.
  • Scalability: Easily scale integration scenarios as your business grows and systems evolve.
  • Flexibility: The suite supports various integration scenarios, from simple point-to-point integrations to complex, multi-cloud architectures.
  • Cost Efficiency: Reduces integration complexity and costs by leveraging a unified platform for all integration needs.

In summary, SAP Integration Suite enables organizations to drive digital transformation by ensuring seamless integration across their entire IT landscape, whether using SAP or third-party applications.

 

4.Boomi to SAP Integration Suite Migration – Tech Envo Framework 

We have worked on multiple Boomi to SAP Integration Suite migration projects and developed a framework and strategy for successful migrations.

 

4.1 Scope of Boomi Integrations to be migrated to SAP Integration Suite:

This is the first step in the migration process: identifying the number of integrations currently running in the Boomi Production Atom environment and ensuring the scope is determined. 

4.2 Document pain points and current environment: 

Talk to the business and technical team supporting the integrations in the BOOMI environment and get a list of pain points and challenges. This is a critical step, and addressing these issues in the SAP Integration Suite after migration is necessary.

Common pain points : 

  1. Poor Integration design 
  2. Poor Integration coding standards 
  3. Not implementing prepackaged integrations
  4. Customizing prepackaged integrations 
  5. Heavy Groovy scripting
  6. Lack of better error handling mechanisms
  7. Lack of documentation
  8. Not using the correct SAP SuccessFactors APIs
  9. Delta Logic is missing 
  10. No encryption
  11. Authentication type issues 
  12. Tightly coupled interfaces - not easy to make updates.

4.3 Identify Integration Patterns: 

Identity integration patterns like Synchronous, Asynchronous, cloud-to-cloud, cloud-to-on-premises, On-premise—on-premise Integration, Scheduled, Real-Time, Type of Systems involved, etc., and create an SAP Integration Suite Integration patterns covering all scenarios. 

  • Synchronous Integration – Real-time data exchange with immediate response—API-to-API communication between SAP SuccessFactors and another system.


                   Fig: Payments canada to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Master Data Sync Integration

Fig: High Resolution flow of Payments canada to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Master Data Sync

  • Asynchronous Integration – Data is sent without waiting for an immediate response.Sending employee updates from SAP SuccessFactors to External Applications.

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SFEC to Metlife Employee Data Sync.

Fig:  High Resolution flow of SAP SFEC to Metlife Employee Data Sync Integration.

  • Cloud-to-Cloud Integration – Data exchange between two cloud applications.Syncing data between SAP SuccessFactors and another cloud-based system(ServiceNow,Okta, Workday,SAP Concur, SAP Fieldglass, S4HANA Cloud.etc)

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to OKTA Data Sync Integration

Fig: High Resolution Flowof SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to OKTA Data Sync Integration

  • Cloud-to-On-Premises Integration – Communication between a cloud-based system and an on-premises system.Triggering updates in an on-premises system from a cloud-based application(S4 HANA OP, SAP ECC, Data Lake OP, SFTP OP).

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SFEC to ShareWorks Employee Data Sync

Fig: High Resolution Flow of SAP SFEC to ShareWorks Employee Data Sync

  • On-Premises to On-Premises Integration – Data exchange between two on-premises systems.Sending employee updates from SAP SuccessFactors to External Applications.

Fig:  Integration Flow of SAP S4HANA or SAP ECC to BankMaster Data Replication

Fig: High Resolution Flow of SAP S4HANA or SAP ECC to BankMaster Data Replication

  • Scheduled Integration – Batch processing executed at defined intervals.Daily batch transfer of employee records from SAP SuccessFactors.

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to Qualtrics Data Sync 

Fig: High Resolution flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to Qualtrics Data Sync 

  • Real-Time Integration – Event-driven or API-based real-time integration.Triggering real-time updates in one system based on events in another.
  • Employee Demographic Data Outbound Integrations

Fig: Active directory(Outbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

  • Employee Data Update Inbound Integrations

Fig: Integration Flow of Active directory(Inbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

Fig: High Resolution Flow Active directory(Inbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

4.4 Create Migration Strategy 

  • Create a detailed strategy covering all aspects of migration:
  1. Deployment Approach and Timelines
  2. Prioritization of interfaces
  3. Technical Architecture
  4. Documenting best practices 

4.5 Map Boomi capabilities with SAP Integration Suite

Identify capabilities used in BOOMI and map which capabilities you need to use in IS. This will give a clear picture of gaps in capabilities and how they can be solved, such as connectors used, API management, Scripting, security, Retry mechanism, error logging capabilities, etc. 

Tech Envo's best practices always include using standard capabilities offered by the SAP integration suite and implementation prepacked integration before building custom integrations.  

4.6 Create Technical Architecture / Design and Development standards

Creating a technical design for each interface is critical. The technical design can be reused depending on the integration type and pattern. Create detailed technical design standards and a framework for the topics below. 

  • Development standards
  • Naming conventions
  • Reusable framework / a common framework (reuse of scripts, security materials, etc)
  • Documentation standards and deliverables
  • Interface development life cycle
  • Interface testing 
  • API best practices 

4.7 Integration Build and Testing

Ensure that the integration consultant working on the integration build has the expertise below. This will help build high-quality integrations. 

  • A clear understanding of SAP SuccessFactors HCM APIs 
  • Excellent hands-on experience in the SAP Integration suite 
  • Experience in Integration build for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
  • Able to understand BOOMI integration code (Boomi platform)

Create a detailed development process for all integrations, align on the development approach, and implement leading practices. This will ensure consultants working on the project follow the same standards and help support the interfaces better.

For example, if you are building delta logic for SAP SuccessFactors HCM data extract, ensure this logic can be reusable across interfaces. Usually, we create a reusable Groovy script. Maintaining the code would be less effort if everyone used the same approach.

4.8 Integrations Testing and comparing BOOMI/SAP Integration Suite Outputs. 

Integration testing is a critical phase of the project. Ensure you document all test cases, check if any are available from the Boomi project, and enhance the same with additional cases. Run the integration in the Boomi environment and SAP Integration suite and compare data. If data matches both platforms, logic integration in SAP integration suits and covers all requirements but follows this process for all scenarios. 

4.9 Deployment in SAP Integration Suite Production 

Once the business approves the integration, plan the deployment in the SAP Integration Suite production environment and schedule as required. Un-deploy or stop the Boomi schedule for the interfaces deployed in the SAP IS prod environment. 

5. Boomi to SAP Cloud Integration - Migration High-Level Decision Flow to determine Integration approach 

The primary goal of the below decision is to leverage prepackaged integrations as much as possible before building a custom integration solution. This approach aligns with the recommendation of SAP's leading practices and SAP clean core principles.

 

 

6. Tech Envo Migration Accelerators

TechEnvo Migration Accelerators include 74+ prepackaged integrations and templates for SAP SuccessFactors HXM in SAP Integration Suite, reusable Framework, and an advanced error-handling framework. Our team is experts in Boomi, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP SuccessFactors HCM solutions, with deep knowledge of SAP SuccessFactors APIs and best practices. With 700+ integrations built using SAP Integration Suite and Boomi, we bring extensive migration experience, ensuring a seamless transition from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite. Our certified professionals have successfully executed multiple Boomi-to-SAP IS migration projects, leveraging industry-leading methodologies.

SF RCM

7. Tech Envo Migration Framework  – SAP SuccessFactors Integration Approach

As part of the migration, 

We develop high-quality integrations in SAP Integration Suite, utilizing its full capabilities to ensure optimal performance. Our approach prioritizes leveraging pre-delivered features with minimal customization by using standard mapping functions and predefined process steps offered by SAP Integration Suite. We also enforce industry-leading development standards aligned with SAP best practices and organizations' leading practices, ensuring consistency, maintainability, and scalability across all integrations.

 

  1. We implement direct API-based Direct integrations if the vendor has built integration and is available in the SAP App Center (e.g., Hire Right, Nakisa, JDMS, SailPoint, Omada IDM, Okta, etc.). Direct integrations avoid the middleware layer and reduce development and support costs.
  2. We implement prepackaged integrations developed by SAP, Tech Envo, or Vendor to reduce development effort and customization.
  3.  We use SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center (IC) if the Integration Center integrations require less effort to build and maintain.
  4. We have built reusable integration templates, which we can use if we need to develop a custom integration. 

 

8. SAP Integration Suite Technical Landscape, Cloud Foundry Environment

  • Cloud Foundry: The Cloud Foundry environment in SAP BTP is an open-source application runtime supporting Java, Node.js, and other languages, ideal for extending SAP products, integrating applications, and building enterprise solutions using SAP-hosted APIs. 
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/technical-landscape-cloud-foundry-environment 

 

9. Deployment Environments of SAP Integration Suite

  • Cloud Integration Runtime: A cloud-based service in SAP Integration Suite, enabling seamless data exchange between cloud and on-premise applications like Boomi Cloud  Atoms.
  • Edge Integration Cell: A hybrid integration runtime in SAP Integration Suite that allows designing in the cloud while running integrations on-premise, equivalent to Boomi On-Premise Atom
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/setting-up-and-managing-edge-integration-cell 
  • Hybrid Deployment Option: Customer can use both Cloud Integration Runtime and Edge Integration Cell - Hybrid deployment option. 

 

10. SFEC to SAP ERP/S4HANA Integrations Migration Approach

We migrate Boomi legacy SAP S4 HANA, SAP ERP HCM, SAP ECC integrations to the BIB Replication Framework for seamless. Employee and Organizational Data replication. Our expertise includes implementing BIB-based integrations for multiple customers, including SAP S/4HANA environments. We have developed accelerators to handle complex replication scenarios, such as dynamic actions, pensioners’ data replication, concurrent employment, and global assignments, ensuring a robust and efficient migration process and no impact to payroll during migration.

 

Migration Key Considerations

1.Connectivity

  • The IP address changes for  Boomi data center (Atoms) and SAP IS are different, so the firewall ports and network config must be updated with new IP range details during migration.
  • Server and Client certificates in the on-premise ERP system must also be updated during migration.
  • Any custom integrations built using SuccessFactors SMTP server for sending emails must be changed to a publicly available SMTP server, as SuccessFactors does not allow external access to its SMTP server.
  • Any locally installed atom needs to be changed to use SAP Cloud Connector (SCC)/Edge Integration. SAP Cloud Connector connects on-premise systems with SAP IS without opening tunnels.

2.Instance Strategy:

  • Depending on the license model, SAP provides two tenants (Test and Production) by default, but we recommend using a three-tier architecture.
  • Multi-tier architecture requires an additional SAP IS tenant, which may involve extra costs.

 

3.Cloud to On-Premise Connectivity

  • On-premise connections via Boomi Local Atom need migration to SAP Cloud Connector or Edge Integration Cell.
  • Proxy tools used in Boomi can still be leveraged in SAP Cloud Integration.

 

4.Interface Mapping: Existing interfaces need to be evaluated based on the following 4 buckets:

  1. Boomi Pre-Packaged to SAP IS Pre-Packaged /Direct API-based integration 
  2. Boomi custom integration to SAP IS a Pre-Packaged integration 
  3. Boomi Custom Integration to SF Integration Center Integrations
  4. Custom Boomi to Custom SAP Integration Suite 

5.Interface Prioritization

  1. Prioritize high-risk integrations such as Payroll, Time, and Benefits 
  2. Ensure SAP IS generates output with the same logic as Boomi.
  3. Validate output files between Boomi and SAP IS to streamline testing.

entry.title: SAP API Management - How to create a Cloud Integration Flow API Provider
entry.content:
This blog is meant to provide a comprehensive guide for users seeking to learn how to create a Cloud Integration Flow API Provider. The solution outlined in this guide employs SAP Integration Suite - API Management and Cloud Integration. It is required for exposing a Cloud Integration iFlow via API Management. Taking advantage of the functionality provided by API Management to govern and monitor the calls made to the Cloud Integration iFlow.

Background Information

During a recent integration project, we were challenged with designing a solution to expose internal Cloud Integration iFlows inside SAP BTP Integration Suite for external access from third party applications. 
To address this, we created an API in API Management that will act as a proxy to the Cloud Integration iFlow. 

From the API Provider menu, the Cloud Integration Flow type is not available for creation:

 

Systems and Platforms 

  • SAP BTP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration and API Management): Integration Platform. 
  • Third Party External Applications 
  • SAP S4HANA 
     

Architecture Diagram 

  1. Third Party External Applications trigger the message 
  2. SAP API Management receives the message 
  3. SAP Cloud Integration iFlow process the message 
  4. SAP S/4HANA receives the message and perform the activities 

 

BTP SAP Process Integration Runtime Instances 

Two SAP Process Integration Runtime instances are required with different plans. One of integration-flow plan and another of api plan. More information about the type of the plan can be found in the following documentation. In both instances, a Service Key is required: 

 Plan integration-flow: 

Plan api: 



Generic iFlow   

A generic and simple iFlow is required to serve as the basis for the Discovery process.  
Create an iFlow called CI_IFLOW and assign a HTTPS Sender adapter with the following configurations: 

After saving, deploy this iFlow. 

 

API Management - API Provider CI_PROVIDER 

 Create an API Provider to get the deployed Cloud Integration iFlows. 

Name: CI_PROVIDER 
Description: API Provider to get the deployed Cloud Integration iFlows 
Type: Cloud Integration 
Cloud Integration Management Host: ….hana.ondemand.com  (Get the value of field “url” from BTP Instance ins-ci-api – default key) 
Port: 443 
Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials 
Client ID: sb-..….6 (Get the value of field “clientid” from BTP Instance ins-ci-api – default key) 
Client Secret: a….= (Get the value of field “clientsecret” from BTP Instance ins-ci-api – default key) 
Token URL: https://....hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token (Get the value of field “tokenurl” from BTP Instance ins-ci-api – default key) 
 

 

 

API Management - API Proxy CI_IFLOW + API Provider CI_IFLOW 

Create an API Proxy called CI_IFLOW. 

Select the Create button: 

Select the API Provider CI_PROVIDER and choose button Discover: 

Select the iflow CI_IFLOW and Next: 

Select the OAuth2ClientCredentials and fill the other required fields 

Client ID: sb-….6 (Get the value of field “clientid” from BTP Instance ins-ci-flow – default-key) 
Client Secret: 8…= (Get the value of field “clientsecret” from BTP Instance ins-ci-flow – default-key) 
Token URL: https://....hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token (Get the value of field “tokenurl” from BTP Instance ins-ci-flow – default-key) 
 

Select the button Done. 

The required fields will be automatically filled.  
Confirm the information and select the button Create: 

The API Proxy CI_IFLOW will be created: 

The API Provider CI_IFLOW will be automatically created with the type of Cloud Integration Flow: 

 

API Management - API Proxy for Integration Flow 

When creating the API Proxy of the Cloud Integration Flow, select the API Provider CI_IFLOW: 

 

Benefits/Advantages of the implemented solution 

This solution utilizes an approach that harnesses the strengths of SAP Cloud Integration + API Management while addressing common challenges associated with authentication and integration. The emphasis on leveraging out-of-the-box capabilities contributes to the efficiency and reliability of the overall solution. 
 


Conclusion

Overall, the solution not only meets the specific requirements of the project but also sets the foundation for a robust and configurable data integration framework. This approach facilitates seamless data flow from external systems to Cloud Integration Flows via API Management. 

I would be more than happy to hear from our SAP Community about similar experiences with this or other integration requirements. 

Please share your feedback in the comments below. I welcome any thoughts you may have on this solution. I encourage you to share similar requirements and how you solved them. 

Thank you for reading! 

Fellipe Mendes

entry.title: General Availability of Data Space Integration, a capability of SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
I am delighted to announce the General Availability of 'Data Space Integration', a capability of SAP Integration Suite 

What Is Data Space Integration?

To meet the challenges of the automotive industry, like high costs and complexity in recall campaigns, or the disruptive effects on supply chains of global crisis like the Covid pandemic, and to enable new use cases like exchanging of carbon emissions amongst business partners to foster sustainability, Catena-X was founded in 2021 by the German government and 28 automotive industry partners. Today the Catena-X data space contains several hundred partners and forms a collaborative eco system that enables a secure and self-sovereign data exchange along the whole value chain.
To participate in this data space, each participant needs a connector. A connector consists of a set of standardized APIs allowing data space participants to provide and consume data within this data space, in a self-sovereign manner. SAP's offering for this connector is Data Space Integration as part of SAP Integration Suite. Via Data Space Integration, SAP customers can discover and negotiate offers of business partners and create own offers to enable the exchange of vital business data without giving away the control of their data. Data Space Integration supports all Catena-X business solutions of SAP and is of course certified for Catena-X.

What can I do with Data Space Integration?

After a guided and intuitive onboarding of Data Space Integration to the Catena-X landscape and the preparation of your data plane, which is in our case an integration flow on Cloud Integration, another capability of SAP Integration Suite, your Data Space Integration connector is ready to participate in the data space. One can participate in the data space by providing data to connected business partners or consuming data from connected business partners.
As a provider, you can register assets that you want to share. You can create access and usage policies, to control who will get access to your data and what are these partners allowed to do with the data. Once you have created those 3 items and bind them to one contract definition, your offer will be visible to the allowed partners.

As a consumer, you can discover the data offerings of other business partners through their catalog, pick a suitable offer and start the negotiation. Once both parties agree on the conditions, you get access to the offered asset and can consume it in your business application. As SAP's business applications are directly integrated with Data Space Integration, all those activities happen seamlessly.

Another key feature of Data Space Integration is the company policy. Data sovereignty does not only imply that you keep in control of your data as a sharing member, but also that you only accept those offers that suit you. With company policies a business admin can control, which offers the company is allowed to consume.

Who can use Data Space Integration?

All existing and new SAP Integration Suite customers with Standard and Premium Edition, BTP Enterprise Agreement and Pay-As-You-Go can enable Data Space Integration in the Integration Suite tenant. The SAP Note 3518230 contains all regional availability information. Visit the Discovery Center for all further license details for SAP Integration Suite.

Kindly stay up-to-date and visit the SAP Integration Suite Roadmap.

entry.title: Writing groovy scripts in VSCode for SAP CI (CPI)
entry.content:
Groovy script development is much easier nowadays with tools like Groovy IDE , which is great for building scripts online. But I always miss the autocomplete in VSCode and also the possibility to use copilot (yes, I like to use AI 🙂), so I decided to build a simple type that helps me handle many of the use cases I work with in Groovy.

From my experience, the most commonly used SAP-specific classes in Groovy are those for getting headers, properties, or the message body, so that’s what I focused on solving — the others I use are usually standard Groovy classes.

Let’s jump into how it looks — the image below shows an example of a script in VSCode:

This gives me a good enough approximation to build locally and transfer to CI afterward.

Hopefully, this is just the beginning. If you think that something else should be added, please let me know. Happy to discuss it.

If you would like to try it, please check the GitHub repo: 19jmrs/SAPCI_GroovySim
You’ll find instructions on how to use it in the GitHub repo, but if you have any questions, feel free to ask.

Cheers!

SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration Open Source

entry.title: SAP Developer News April 24th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

PODCAST: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/04/24/sap-developer-news-april-24th-2025/

SAP Build news

  • SAP Build included with SAP S/4HANA Cloud https://news.sap.com/2025/04/sap-build-sap-s4hana-cloud-simplify-development/
  • SAP Cloud ERP Private Package Accelerates Transformation https://news.sap.com/2025/04/sap-cloud-erp-private-package-accelerate-transformation/
  • Introducing the New Project Creation Wizard within SAP Build Lobby https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/introducing-the-new-project-creation-wizard-within-sap-build-lobby/ba-p/14079038

New Basic Trial available

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/new-basic-trial-available-get-your-hands-on-enterprise-automation-with-sap/ba-p/14075170
  • Basic trial https://www.sap.com/products/try-sap.html

Event Mesh bridge and Developer Hub

  • Event Mesh bridge in SAP Integration Suite: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/event-mesh-bridge-in-sap-integration/ba-p/14076056
  • Activating Event Mesh bridge: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/activating-event-mesh-bridge
  • Enable SAP Developer Hub as central Event Catalog: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/enable-sap-developer-hub-as-central-event-catalog/ba-p/14076495

 

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP Build news

2:22 New Basic Trial available

4:52 Event Mesh bridge and Developer Hub

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Into] This is the SAP Developer News for April 24th, 2025.

[DJ] In the early days of SAP R2, key developer tools were separate from the core ERP offering. When we moved to the client-server architecture of SAP R3, the key tools were built-in, part of the experience. Well, today with SAP S4 HANA Cloud, there's a return to batteries included approach because all SAP build capabilities are now included. This means that core SAP facilities for extending and personalizing SAP S4 HANA Cloud systems are right there for customers to use. Apart from a lower TCO and a simpler license model. It's basically the right tools for building extensions and integrations and ready-made content, Joule capabilities and more. It's all there from the get-go. What's more, SAP HANA Cloud is also included. Plus, you can check out all the details in the article linked in the description. By the way, this bundling is also included with SAP cloud ERP private packages and there's another article linked in the description that has more details on that and while i'm here talking about sap build there's a new project creation wizard in the sap build lobby this wizard has been redesigned based upon user research including interviews with different user groups the new design has basically a sort of a new intense based navigation rather than focusing on what tools you want to use it focuses on what you want to build it's got an updated user experience and also the categories the project categories have been reorganized to make things hopefully easier for you and the feedback doesn't stop because there's a feedback facility in the top right that you can use to send your feedback on this new wizard. So happy building and send feedback if you need to.

[Thomas] In this new segment, I'd like to tell you about the new Enterprise Automation Basic Trial. But first, a few words about what is the basic trial, particularly in the context of SAP BTP. So we have two types of trials available for SAP BTP. We have the basic trial and we have the advanced trial. Now, the advanced trial is essentially the BTP trial that you've had access to for several years now. It takes your existing SAP user account and grants it access to its own global and sub-account in SAP BTP. There you have administrative rights and the ability to set up and use many different services and capabilities of SAP PTP. You have full access to that account and can explore many different scenarios. The basic trial, by comparison, we grant you access to an existing environment. So you don't have to wait for a new environment to be provisioned and spin up and configured. You get immediate access and it isn't tied to your existing user account you're given a unique username and password with which to log in now when you access this basic trial environment it's pre-configured and set up for whatever scenario you're accessing and there's a limited number of scenarios so far but the beauty of it is that you get immediate access and certain things are pre-configured and ready for you to use. So if you're looking to try out a new capability in the easiest way possible, then the basic trial is a great way to get started. But on the other hand, if you want to dive deep and really explore a capability, the advanced trial is a better option. Now back to today's topic, which is the new enterprise automation basic trial. And this focuses on the enterprise automation scenario and includes capabilities like SAP BTP integration suite, Signavio, and SAP build business process automation. And you get all those tools and capabilities pre-configured in that environment. So if this is something that you want to explore, particularly how these different capabilities interact and work with one another, then check out this new basic trial. All the links and information are in the description below.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP Developers! So you've embraced event-driven architectures, and you're using the Event Mesh capability in SAP Integration Suite, but you're experiencing a large volume of events, and now you're looking at adopting Advanced Event Mesh to scale your event-driven architecture practice? Well, did you know that it is now possible to connect the broker part of the Event Mesh capability with your instance of Advanced Event Mesh? This is called the Event Mesh Bridge. You just configure the details of an AEM broker in the Event Mesh Bridge and set the topics that you're interested in forwarding. An AEM will start receiving all those events. Awesome, isn't it? Well, but with all those events going around in your landscape, it'll be great to have a central place to document them all, right? Fortunately, you can do this within SAP Integration Suite also. via the Developer Hub, which is part of the API management capability. You can import an async API specification, add them to a product, and make them publicly available to the developers in your organization. Check out Florian's blog post for more details. Ciao
